title: Xenial Turkish Semolina Pilaf
description: A bitter yet juicy Turkish pilaf that brings a cozy feel with semolina and pickles, crafted without onion or garlic.
instructions: In a saucepan, heat the olive oil over medium heat until shimmering.
Add the ground cumin, coriander, sumac, and salt; stir continuously for 30 seconds to toast the spices.
Pour in the semolina, stirring constantly, until it turns lightly golden and smells nutty.
Add the water and vegetable broth, bring the mixture to a gentle boil, then reduce heat to low.
Stir in the lemon juice, then cover and simmer for 10 minutes, allowing the semolina to absorb the liquid.
Remove the saucepan from heat and fluff the pilaf gently with a wooden spoon.
Fold in the diced cucumber pickles, dill, and mint, mixing until evenly distributed.
Taste and adjust seasoning with additional salt or lemon juice if needed.
Transfer the pilaf to a serving dish and top with a drizzle of olive oil.
Let it rest for 5 minutes before serving, giving the flavors a chance to meld.
